generations.generated_text:
Let's evaluate each option step by step:

A. StereoSet and CrowS-Pairs both use word-filling (fill-in-the-blank) methods. CrowS-Pairs uses two sentences with minimal differences, one stereotypical and one anti-stereotypical, and measures preference. StereoSet uses intrasentence and intersentence tests with three options: stereotype, anti-stereotype, and unrelated. The score is based on preference for stereotype over anti-stereotype, not proportion of choices including stereotype. So A is incorrect.

B. ETHOS does not use irrelevant options; it classifies hate speech as "hate" or "non-hate". StereoSet does include an unrelated option. CrowS-Pairs does not include irrelevant options either — it has only stereotypical and anti-stereotypical options. So B is incorrect.

C. ETHOS does not require a yes/no answer. It classifies as hate or non-hate. The model classifies based on content, not a binary yes/no. So C is incorrect.

D. All three articles (CrowS-Pairs, StereoSet, ETHOS) cover race, religion, and sexism (gender bias). CrowS-Pairs has race, religion, age, gender, etc. StereoSet has gender, profession, race, religion. ETHOS has race, gender, religion. So D is correct.

D
